question: Develop a `DynamicArray` class that manages an array of integers on the heap. Include a constructor that takes size, a destructor that deallocates memory, and a method `setElement(index, value)`.

test_cases.expected:
Test Case 1: Basic functionality and out-of-bounds write (Robustness Issue)
Element at index 0: 10
Element at index 4: 50
Attempting to write out of bounds (index 5)...
Value written to index 5 (may be garbage or crash program): (throws std::out_of_range or crashes due to UB)

Test Case 2: Demonstrating Rule of Three violation (Robustness Issue)
Original array elements: 11 22 33 
Copied array element at index 0 after modification: 99
Original array element at index 0 (also changed due to shallow copy): 99
(Program terminates with a double-free error or other memory corruption when objects are destroyed)

errors: Missing copy constructor and copy assignment operator (Rule of Three violation), leading to shallow copies and double-free errors when objects are copied.
Lack of bounds checking in `setElement` method, allowing out-of-bounds memory access and resulting in undefined behavior or crashes.
